claim: Knowledge graphs of open-source repositories enable AI coding assistants to detect and self-correct hallucinations by querying the actual codebase structure instead of relying on documentation alone
actionable: Index open-source libraries (e.g., Pydantic AI) into a knowledge graph (using Neo4j), then give your AI coding assistant tools to query it and detect invalid function calls before they cause debugging loops
source_title: AI Code That Fixes Itself (An MCP You Can Try Now)

claim: Hallucination detection via knowledge graphs can verify not just function existence but also function parameters; invalid parameters can be caught deterministically without LLM analysis
actionable: Use deterministic code (not LLMs) to query your knowledge graph and check both function names and their parameter lists; this catches parameter hallucinations instantly
source_title: AI Code That Fixes Itself (An MCP You Can Try Now)

claim: Indexing a GitHub repository into a knowledge graph takes less than 30 seconds and requires no LLM; deterministic parsing of files creates a queryable graph of repositories, classes, functions, and attributes
actionable: Use deterministic code to parse GitHub repos and build knowledge graphs; this is faster and more cost-effective than LLM-based indexing for code
source_title: AI Code That Fixes Itself (An MCP You Can Try Now)

claim: An AI coding assistant using knowledge graphs with proper MCP tools can build a correct Pydantic AI agent on the first try, reducing hallucinations from typical debugging loops to zero
actionable: Set up a Crawl4AI RAG MCP server with knowledge graphs for your target libraries, connect it to Claude Code or similar, and use hallucination-checking tools in your prompts
source_title: AI Code That Fixes Itself (An MCP You Can Try Now)

claim: For specific domains (frontend building with Lovable/Bolt, browser automation with Stagehand/Director), specialized tools with built-in self-correction are superior to general MCP servers
actionable: For frontend apps, use Lovable or Bolt.new instead of trying to code with Claude; for browser automation, use Stagehand or Director instead of building your own
source_title: AI Code That Fixes Itself (An MCP You Can Try Now)

claim: MCP server tools for knowledge graphs can be agent-agnostic; the same MCP server works with Claude Code, Gemini, and other agents through standard tool protocols
actionable: Build MCP servers with knowledge graph queries that are agent-agnostic; this allows any AI coding assistant to benefit from your knowledge graphs
source_title: AI Code That Fixes Itself (An MCP You Can Try Now)
